info.title: Amazon Textract
info.description: Amazon Textract detects and analyzes text in documents and converts it into machine-readable text. This is the API Reference for Amazon Textract.

post.description: Analyzes an input document for relationships between detected items. The types of information returned are as follows: Form data (key-value pairs), Tables and table cells, Text (lines and words), Signatures, Queries results, and Layout elements.
Synchronously analyzes an input document for financially related relationships between text. Information is returned as ExpenseDocuments and seperate LineItemGroups.
Analyzes identity documents for relevant information. This information is extracted and returned as IdentityDocumentFields, which records both the normalized field and value of the extracted text.

Detects text in the input document. Amazon Textract can detect lines of text and the words that make up a line of text. The input document must be in one of the following image formats: JPEG, PNG, PDF, or TIFF.
Starts the asynchronous analysis of an input document for relationships between detected items such as key-value pairs, tables, and selection elements.
Gets the results for an Amazon Textract asynchronous operation that analyzes text in a document. You start asynchronous text analysis by calling StartDocumentAnalysis.
Starts the asynchronous detection of text in a document. Amazon Textract can detect lines of text and the words that make up a line of text.
Gets the results for an Amazon Textract asynchronous operation that detects text in a document. You start asynchronous text detection by calling StartDocumentTextDetection.
Starts the asynchronous analysis of invoices or receipts for data like contact information, items purchased, and vendor names.
Gets the results for an Amazon Textract asynchronous operation that analyzes invoices and receipts.
Starts the classification and analysis of an input document for lending-related information.
Gets the results for an Amazon Textract asynchronous operation that analyzes text in a lending document.
Gets summarized results for the StartLendingAnalysis operation with document grouping and signature detection.
Creates an adapter, which can be fine-tuned for enhanced performance on user provided documents.
Creates a new version of an adapter. Needs a dataset and compute resources to train.
Deletes an Amazon Textract adapter. Takes an AdapterId and deletes the adapter specified by the ID.
Deletes an Amazon Textract adapter version.
Gets configuration information for an adapter specified by an AdapterId.
Gets configuration information for the specified adapter version.
Lists all adapters that match the specified filtration criteria.
Lists all versions of an adapter that meet the specified filtration criteria.
Lists all tags for an Amazon Textract resource.
Adds one or more tags to the specified resource.
Removes any tags with the specified keys from the specified resource.
Update the configuration for an adapter. FeatureTypes configurations cannot be updated.

Document.description: The input document, either as bytes or as an S3 object reference.

Bytes.description: A blob of base64-encoded document bytes. Max size 10 MB.

S3Object.description: The S3 bucket name and file name that identifies the document.

Bucket.description: The name of the S3 bucket.

Name.description: The file name of the input document.

Version.description: The version of the S3 object.

DocumentLocation.description: The Amazon S3 bucket that contains the document to be processed.

Pages.description: The number of pages detected in the document.

Block.description: A Block represents items that are recognized in a document within a group of pixels close to each other.

Confidence.description: The confidence score that Amazon Textract has in the accuracy of the recognized text.

Text.description: The word or line of text recognized by Amazon Textract.

RowIndex.description: The row in which a table cell is located.

ColumnIndex.description: The column in which a table cell appears.

RowSpan.description: The number of rows that a table cell spans.

ColumnSpan.description: The number of columns that a table cell spans.

Id.description: The identifier for the recognized text.

Relationships.description: A list of relationship objects that describe how blocks are related to each other.

Page.description: The page on which a block was detected.

Relationship.description: Information about how blocks are related to each other.

Ids.description: An array of IDs for related blocks.

Geometry.description: Information about where the following items are located on a document page.

Polygon.description: Within the bounding box, a fine-grained polygon around the recognized item.

BoundingBox.description: The bounding box around the detected page, text, key-value pair, table, table cell, or selection element.

Point.description: The X and Y coordinates of a point on a document page.

Text.description: Question that Amazon Textract will apply to the document.

Alias.description: Alias attached to the query, for ease of location.

Pages.description: Pages is a parameter that the user inputs to specify which pages to apply a query to.

AdapterId.description: A unique identifier for the adapter resource.

FlowDefinitionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the flow definition.

HumanLoopActivationConditions.description: JSON conditions for when a human review is triggered.

SNSTopicArn.description: The Amazon SNS topic that Amazon Textract posts the completion status to.

RoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an IAM role that gives Amazon Textract publishing permissions to the Amazon SNS topic.

S3Bucket.description: The name of the bucket your output will go to.

S3Prefix.description: The prefix of the object key that the output will be saved to.

ClientRequestToken.description: The idempotent token used to identify the start request.

KMSKeyId.description: The KMS key used to encrypt the inference results.

JobTag.description: An identifier that you specify to be included in the completion notification.

JobId.description: The identifier for the document text detection job.
A unique identifier for the text-detection job.

MaxResults.description: The maximum number of results to return per paginated call.

NextToken.description: Pagination token for retrieving the next set of results.

AdapterName.description: The name to be assigned to the adapter being created.
